homepage.title: Steve Reed | Labour & Co-operative Party
homepage.meta_description: A member of the Labour and Co-operative Party, he has been the Member of Parliament (MP) for Streatham and Croydon North, formerly Croydon North since 2012.
headings: h2: I'm The labour member of parliament for streatham and croydon north. It is my honour to serve you. the work of changing britain has begun.
h2: Get the latest from steve reed
homepage.text_excerpt: Steve Reed | Labour & Co-operative Party STEVE REED MP Please contact my office at Steve [email protected] for enquiries and casework. I'm The labour member of parliament for streatham and croydon north. It is my honour to serve you. the work of changing britain has begun. Steve Reed MP Labour Member of Parliament for Streatham and Croydon North About Steve Subscribe to stay up to date with Steve’s work Name Email Post Code Subscribe Latest News Admiral Casino Application Paused Steve Reed Petition: No 24-Hour Gambling in Crystal Palace Steve Reed How Labour’s Autumn 2025 Budget Impacts You Steve Reed Life Gets a Little Easier for Working Families Steve Reed Bring the Overground to Streatham! Steve Reed What matters to you? – One year after Streatham and Croydon North voted for change. Steve Reed See More AT A GLANCE Steve has dealt with over 120,000 individual cases for local residents who asked in Helping residents - one of the highest levels for any MP. — Helping residents Steve is the first Labour MP to pass a major Act of Parliament from opposition in 22 years, protecting mental health patients from violent restraint of the kind that killed 21-year-old South Norwood graduate Seni Lewis. — Seni’s Law Steve has held senior positions on Labour’s front bench - he’s currently Shadow Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs. Previously, he has been Labour’s spokesperson for Communities and Local Government; Justice; Children and Families; Civil Society; and Crime Prevention. — Front Bench Steve led the successful campaign to save Croydon University Hospital from closure, and secured £20 million investment in a new A&E. — NHS Steve pioneered a new approach to tackling crime at source, successfully cutting violent youth crime in Lambeth by a third in just 18 months. — Knife Crime Steve was the first MP in the country to get flammable, Grenfell-style cladding stripped from a local housing block, and brought thousands of new affordable homes to Lambeth before becoming an MP. — Housing Steve led the transformation of Lambeth’s services for vulnerable children from worst-rated to best-rated in the country before becoming an MP. — Vulnerable Children Steve helped set up Repowering - now the UK’s biggest community owned sustainable energy company - and has called for Croydon Council to set up its own clean energy cooperative — Energy, Environment and Climate STEVE REED Steve Reed is the Labour MP for Streatham and Croydon North. He was first elected in a by-election in 2012 having previously served as leader of Lambeth council from 2006-12. He was the Secretary of State for Food, Rural Affairs and Environment following Labour’s election victory in 2024. In September 2025, he was appointed the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government. pages.title: About - Steve Reed MP
headings: h2: Get the latest from steve reed
pages.text_excerpt: About - Steve Reed MP ABOUT STEVE Labour Member of Parliament for Streatham and Croydon North, and Secretary of State for Housing, Communities & Local Government. My Track Record Subscribe to stay up to date with Steve’s work Name Email Post Code Subscribe In Parliament Steve Reed is the Labour MP for Streatham and Croydon North. He was first elected in a by-election in 2012, and has been re-elected at four general elections since. Following Labour’s election victory in 2024, Prime Minister Keir Starmer appointed Steve to serve as Secretary of State for the Environment, Food and Rural Affairs. In the September 2025 Cabinet reshuffle, he was appointed Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government. He was previously Labour’s spokesperson for Justice, and before that for Communities and Local Government. Since becoming an MP, Steve has worked hard for local people. He has helped local people with over 120,000 individual cases. Got flammable cladding stripped from a local housing block Led the campaign that saved Croydon University Hospital from closure Demanded action on fly tipping and litter by the local council SENI'S LAW In 2018, Steve became the first Labour MP in 22 years to pass a major new Act of Parliament from opposition. Seni’s Law protects people with mental ill health from violent restraint andtackles deaths in custody. The law is named after 21-year-old South Norwood graduate Seni Lewis, who tragically died after he was subject to abusive restraint in 2010. Before Parliament Steve was Leader of Lambeth Council for over six years before he was elected to Parliament. During this time Steve introduced the Living Wage to tackle low pay, invested in better council housing, cut violent youth crime by a third, and transformed Lambeth’s children’s services from worst to best-rated in the country – all while freezing council tax to help residents with the cost of living. Steve grew up in a working-class family on the outskirts of London. His dad worked in a print factory, his mum was a cleaner, and he was the first member of his family to go to university. He began his career in education and business publishing, and was elected branch secretary of his trade union where he won improved parental leave for mums and dads with new-born babies. pages.title: Petition: No 24-Hour Gambling in Crystal Palace - Steve Reed MP
pages.h1: Petition: No 24-Hour Gambling in Crystal Palace
pages.meta_description: Admiral Casino, an adult gambling centre on Westow Hill is currently open from 9am- 11pm. They have recently submitted a planning application to keep the centre open 24 hours, 7 days a week. There have been concerns raised about what this change will cause in terms of anti social behaviour and the character and identity of The Triangle. Working with the local Labour team, I am urging Croydon Council to take the above factors into consideration. If you agree, please sign my petition.
headings: h3: Petition: No 24-Hour Gambling in Crystal Palace
h3: How Labour’s Autumn 2025 Budget Impacts You
pages.text_excerpt: Petition: No 24-Hour Gambling in Crystal Palace - Steve Reed MP Campaigns Petition: No 24-Hour Gambling in Crystal Palace Steve Reed February 16, 2026 Admiral Casino, an adult gambling centre on Westow Hill is currently open from 9am- 11pm. They have recently submitted a planning application to keep the centre open 24 hours, 7 days a week. There have been concerns raised about what this change will cause in terms of anti social behaviour, and the character and identity of The Triangle. Working with the local Labour team, I am urging Croydon Council to take the above factors into consideration. If you agree, please sign my petition by clicking here . Share Share Tweet Latest News Admiral Casino Application Paused Petition: No 24-Hour Gambling in Crystal Palace How Labour’s Autumn 2025 Budget Impacts You At Facebook-square Twitter Instagram Steve Reed is the Labour MP for Streatham and Croydon North and Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government. In 2018 his private member’s bill on reducing violent mental health restraint became law. In June 2019 he launched Labour’s civil society strategy outlining radical plans to empower citizens and communities. Steve chairs the Cooperative Councils Innovation Network, co-chairs the All-Party Parliamentary Group for London, was Leader of Lambeth Council 2006-12 where he led the council’s children’s services to become best-rated in the country and pioneered the public-health approach to tackling violent youth crime. He worked in publishing for 16 years and was an elected trade union branch secretary. pages.title: Contact - Steve Reed MP
headings: h2: Get the latest from steve reed
pages.text_excerpt: Contact - Steve Reed MP Get In Touch WE ARE EXPERIENCING TECHNICAL ISSUES WITH THIS FORM AT PRESENT. TO CONTACT STEVE, PLEASE EMAIL: [email protected] Thank you for your patience. If you are a resident of Streatham and Croydon North, Steve is always happy to raise your concerns with any relevant organisation or service provider. He can ask the organisation to investigate your issue or reconsider a decision and he will always ask that your concerns are looked into fairly. However, MPs do not have control over the final decision. Submit Message . Steve’s here to help For council issues, it’s best to raise your concerns initially with your local councillors. They are your elected representatives on the council and have authority over the decisions it takes and what it does. If you have contacted your councillors and have not received a response within 10 working days, please write to Steve and he will contact your councillors directly on your behalf. You can find out who your ward councillors are here In some cases you may require professional legal or financial advice. MPs are not qualified to provide this and nor can we recommend professionals for you to contact. If you are unsure of who to contact, Steve may be able to signpost you to a relevant advice organisation. You can find a list of organisations that provide free, professional advice relating to immigration, financial or employment issues here Advice surgeries If you would like to make an appointment at one of Steve’s advice surgeries, please get in touch via email or post. It is often faster for Steve and his office to deal with queries by phone or email, but Steve holds regular advice surgeries for constituents who prefer to meet in person. If you wish to make an in-person appointment, please include your full postal address and details of the issue you would like to discuss. Email : s [email protected] Telephone : 020 7219 7297 Post : Rt Hon Steve Reed MP, House of Commons, London SW1A 0AA Office standards Steve’s office receives over 100 emails, phone calls and letters from constituents every day. We do our best to reply as quickly as we can, but Steve has no power to force service providers to change decisions that constituents are unhappy with. Steve’s staff will always treat constituents with professionalism and respect, and he kindly asks that in return members of the public show the same consideration for his staff. Steve will not tolerate rude or aggressive behaviour towards his staff. Get the latest from steve reed Name Email Post Code Subscribe * We don’t share your personal info with anyone.
